title: Kale Pesto

overview.description: A vibrant green pesto made with kale, basil, walnuts and Parmesan. An economical, nutritious alternative to traditional basil pesto that freezes exceptionally well.

method: Bring a pan of salted water to the boil.
Drain and immediately cool in iced water.
Drain well and squeeze out as much water as possible.
Toast the walnuts in a dry frying pan over a medium heat until lightly golden.
Place the kale, basil, walnuts, Parmesan and garlic into a food processor.
With the processor running, slowly pour in the olive oil.
Add the lemon juice and blend until the pesto reaches your preferred consistency.
Transfer to jars or portion into freezer containers.

learning.did-you-know: Modern pestos are often made with seasonal leafy greens, allowing the classic technique to be adapted while keeping the fresh, vibrant flavour.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How blanching preserves the bright green colour of leafy vegetables and how to make a smooth homemade pesto.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A healthier, more economical pesto that makes excellent use of kale while still delivering the fresh flavour of basil.
chapman-tips: Blanching the kale keeps the pesto bright green and removes any bitterness.
Toast the walnuts for a deeper flavour.
Freeze in approximately 2 tablespoon portions for quick meals.
A thin layer of olive oil over the top helps preserve the colour during storage.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in silicone ice cube trays or small containers for up to 6 months.
notes.leftovers: Excellent stirred through pasta, spread on sandwiches, spooned over roasted vegetables or served with homemade gnocchi.
